hypothesis: If catalytic surface composition design is reformulated as a conditional 2D shell-wise generation problem using a chemically informed latent space and diffusion model, then the resulting framework (CompGen) will generate chemically valid and energetically targeted surface compositions with greater distributional fidelity and constraint compliance than a chemically-agnostic baseline, because encoding domain-specific periodic table relationships into the latent space provides structured priors that guide the generative model toward physically meaningful compositional regions.
